5. And all of this happens over one weekend?

Yeah, we’re pretty back.

thread_tweets.text:
oh yea, no doubt. Like I think figure has the coolest robot for example, but they can't scale it yet. the only person who can really scale anything to the extent we are thinking is elon bc he already has the money, and infrastructure to do it 

and especially the generalized ones bc the spaces they have to navigate is extremely tough. for example. having one in your home requires that when it makes your coffee, it doesnt break your favorite mug. or it can navigate stairs, complex workspaces, manipualte objects with varying force etc. 

the bottlenecks etc yes, they are real, and when this scales they obviously benefit and with higher margins etc. especially on the software side (let me shill bb here)

thread_tweets.text:
urban development:
-the electric cars are more advanced than teslas
-power bank infra has wide distribution so you never run out of phone battery, just check out a battery pack from a vending machine like a library card
-delivery logistics are top tier. get whatever you need wherever you need it. ex: get medicine delivered to your table while eating dinner at a restaurant. 
-advanced national high speed rail system makes ground travel competitive with air across large parts of the country 

trends with cn apes I’ve met:
-many new members are young and testing the waters by collecting mutants
-extremely active to the point where people can misdiagnose reply speed and activity with bots. they’re not, I’ve met them irl, they’re just that engaged on the TL
-younger entrants aiming to acquire bayc as a longer term goal
-bigger holders tend to be much reserved/quieter/chill than their western counterparts

many more observations than this, but am still reflecting

tweets.text:
As we leave the historically weakest week of the year behind us today, we now enter the 12-Day Midyear Rally: the last three trading days of June and the first nine trading days of July.

Since 1985, the Nasdaq has returned an average of +2.5% during this period and has finished higher 78% of the time.
tweets.quoted_tweet_text:
Today is the final day of what has historically been considered the weakest week of the year for the stock market.

If you’re feeling nervous, this is probably not the day to panic or make emotional decisions.

History suggests that seasonality becomes more favorable starting

thread_tweets.text: @demon386 When many people believe something, it’s important to pay attention to it, or at least be aware of it.
thread_tweets.full_text: @demon386 When many people believe something, it’s important to pay attention to it, or at least be aware of it.

thread_tweets.text:
Everything depends on the context. Markets are case specific.

In this case, after one of the historically weakest weeks of the year, a relief rally makes sense. Being contrarian doesn’t mean automatically betting against the crowd. Most of the time, the crowd is actually right. The edge comes from recognizing the situations when it’s wrong, not assuming it’s always wrong.

tweets.text: Life is short
tweets.quoted_tweet_text:
I was driving home from the store today and imagining life remapped to a single day.

Both my parents are gone now and I've been reflecting more on mortality. It feels so unreal and intangible. It's 4:30 pm for me, I still have time left but it's finite. It all goes by so fast https://t.co/Y8YOCOr9pc

tweets.text: On Friday I did a test drive with Tesla FSD in The Netherlands. The Dutch package was set a bit too conservative for my liking, and not paying attention for a few seconds led to a strike, but the car drove flawlessly. If I’d live here I’d sell my car and get a Model Y with FSD.

tweets.text: I don’t really see the problem. Using AI to write a PhD thesis makes the process more efficient. It doesn’t mean you don’t understand the subject matter. Nobody reads it anyway, but just prompt it well so AI doesn’t create slop.
tweets.quoted_tweet_text:
Update: After scanning almost 23,000 dissertations, I can now say... it's REALLY bad out there.

More than 1-in-5 dissertations uses AI nowadays, much of the time to do all of the writing. https://t.co/RhJUToL65T
tweets.full_text: I don’t really see the problem. Using AI to write a PhD thesis makes the process more efficient. It doesn’t mean you don’t understand the subject matter. Nobody reads it anyway, but just prompt it well so AI doesn’t create slop.
